page: Microsoft Azure E96-48ds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the EDSv5 family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, with 24 physical cores and hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 14.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes a 3686 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. No GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark results show strong performance in memory-intensive and data-processing tasks. The instance achieved a strong rating in large-block memory bandwidth, with read-only speeds of 261.74 GB/sec and write speeds of 130.97 GB/sec. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reached 228.53 GB/sec, and Passmark database operations scored 23765.97, both categorized as strong. CPU-bound tasks, such as stress-ng multi-core performance and Geekbench multi-core, fall within the average range. However, large-scale LLM text generation is a notable bottleneck, with 70B model inference processing at a poor rate of 2.41 tokens/sec due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E96-48ds_v5 offers high resource density, particularly in memory capacity and local storage, making it a viable option for workloads that require substantial RAM without scaling up vCPU counts excessively. The combination of 672.0 GB of RAM, 3.6 TB of local storage, and strong database operation benchmarks makes this instance highly suitable for large relational databases, in-memory caching systems, data analytics, and enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E96-48ds_v5 is a memory-optimized server instance from the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor operating at 2.8 GHz, delivering 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores. The instance features a substantial 672.0 GB of RAM alongside 3686 GB of local storage, with Premium Storage capabilities. Performance benchmarks highlight strong capabilities in memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds of 261.74 GB/sec and write speeds of 130.97 GB/sec, as well as strong database operations. While CPU performance remains average and large LLM inference is limited by the absence of a GPU, the high memory-to-core ratio provides excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads. This server is best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, and intensive data processing applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48ds_v5 is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 3686 GB local storage, delivering strong database and memory bandwidth performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48ds_v5 is a memory-optimized server with 48 Intel vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 3.6 TB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and storage instance featuring 672 GB RAM and 3.6 TB local disk for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 2.8 GHz clock speed
672.0 GB of system memory providing a high 14.0 GB per core ratio
3686 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong memory bandwidth with 261.74 GB/sec read and 130.97 GB/sec write speeds
Optimized for large relational databases, in-memory caches, and data analytics
